name: Jerome Powell
role: Fed Chair (term as Chair expires May 15, 2026 — 21 days; Governor through Jan 2028)
quote: The tariff effects will include higher inflation and slower growth — significantly larger than anticipated. We are well positioned to wait for greater clarity before adjusting policy. [Harvard, Mar 30 2026]. NOTE: Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, 2026 — now just 21 days away as of April 24. Kevin Warsh's confirmation is stalled by Sen. Tillis's hold. The FOMC meets April 28-29 under Powell — his last scheduled meeting as Chair — with Michigan sentiment at all-time lows, Brent crude at $107, and CPI at 3.3%. A successor Fed Chair has not been confirmed, creating the most acute Fed leadership uncertainty since 1987.

name: Christine Lagarde
quote: The volatility we have seen in global markets this week is a direct consequence of trade policy uncertainty. The ECB is monitoring conditions closely. A sharp deterioration in financial conditions or growth could prompt a reassessment of our policy stance. European financial stability remains robust. [Apr 4, 2026]

name: Scott Bessent
quote: The market selloff is an adjustment to a structurally stronger American economy. Short-term volatility is the price of restoring American manufacturing competitiveness and ending decades of unfair trade practices. We are building something durable. [Apr 6, 2026]

name: Kristalina Georgieva
role: Managing Director, IMF (2nd term from Oct 2024)
quote: Our April 2026 WEO confirms global growth at 3.1% in our reference scenario — with an adverse scenario of 2.5% and severe scenario of 2.0% if the conflict continues. Headline inflation has risen to 4.4% globally. The IMF is providing $20-50 billion in emergency support to energy-importing developing economies. We call on all countries to coordinate on debt restructuring, quota reform, and to resist the fragmentation of the global trading system. [IMF Spring Meetings closing, Apr 18 2026]

name: Li Qiang
role: Premier, People's Republic of China (since March 2023)
quote: China's manufacturing PMI returning to expansion in March confirms the resilience of our economy. We are committed to working constructively with the United States on trade, rare earths, and shared economic stability. A stable bilateral relationship benefits all.

name: Kazuo Ueda
quote: We will continue our efforts to achieve the price stability target in a sustainable and stable manner. The timing and pace of adjusting monetary accommodation will depend on economic, price, and financial conditions.

name: Andrew Bailey
quote: A gradual and careful approach to removing policy restraint remains appropriate given the need to assess how persistent above-target inflation will be, against a backdrop of global uncertainty.

name: Ajay Banga
quote: Developing countries are facing a convergence of challenges — high debt, elevated food and energy costs, and now trade disruption. The World Bank stands ready to support resilient and inclusive growth.

name: Pan Gongsheng
role: Governor, People's Bank of China (since July 2023)
quote: The People's Bank of China will strengthen counter-cyclical adjustment, maintain liquidity at reasonably ample levels, and support the real economy. We have ample tools to deal with external shocks.

name: Gita Gopinath
role: First Deputy Managing Director, IMF; former Chief Economist
quote: Trade fragmentation is a significant drag on long-run productivity and growth. Economies that become more closed lose the gains from specialization and economies of scale that have underpinned decades of prosperity.

name: Christopher Waller
role: Federal Reserve Governor; dissented for rate cut at Jan 2026 FOMC
quote: I believe the policy rate is still meaningfully restrictive and that we can gradually lower it. The risks to achieving our dual-mandate goals are more balanced than our current stance reflects.

name: Isabel Schnabel
role: Executive Board Member, ECB (Germany); inflation hawk
quote: We should not be complacent about inflation persistence. While disinflation has made significant progress, wage growth and services inflation require us to remain vigilant. Cutting too aggressively risks undoing years of credibility-building.

name: Donald Trump
role: US President (since Jan 20, 2025); primary trade policy driver
quote: China had their chance to remove the 34% retaliation and they refused. So now it's 104%, effective immediately. They want to fight — fine, we have all the leverage. [Apr 9, 2026]. NOTE: Trump-Xi summit confirmed May 14-15, 2026 in Beijing — first Trump-Xi meeting since 2017. Agenda: agriculture purchases, rare earth concessions, fentanyl enforcement, and potential tariff reduction framework. IEEPA-era tariffs struck down Feb 20; Section 122 at 8.9% overall rate remains.

name: Janet Yellen
role: Former US Treasury Secretary (Jan 2021–Jan 2025); Former Fed Chair
quote: We achieved something rare in economic history: bringing inflation down from 40-year highs without triggering a recession. The soft landing was not guaranteed — it required skill, patience, and some good fortune with supply chains.

name: François Villeroy de Galhau
role: Governor, Banque de France; ECB Governing Council dove
quote: The eurozone cannot afford complacency in the face of tariff-driven stagnation. With inflation near target, the ECB must prioritize economic momentum. Europe needs its own fiscal tools to complement monetary easing.

name: Philip Jefferson
quote: Energy prices are a key source of uncertainty in the current inflation outlook. Sustained increases in oil prices could temporarily push up inflation and complicate our path back to the 2% target. We are monitoring developments carefully. [Mar 26, 2026]

name: Austan Goolsbee
role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago (since Jan 2023)
quote: Stagflation is the worst situation for the central bank because raising rates doesn't solve it, cutting rates doesn't solve it, and staying the same doesn't solve it. The longer this goes where we never got to see the decrease in inflation, that starts pushing rate cuts out of '26 — potentially into 2027. [Semafor World Economy 2026 Conference, Apr 14, 2026]

name: Kevin Warsh
role: Nominee, Federal Reserve Chair; Former Fed Governor (2006–2011); Senior Fellow, Stanford Hoover Institution
quote: I will not be the President's 'sock puppet.' The Federal Reserve's independence is non-negotiable. The president never asked me to pre-determine, commit, fix, or decide on any interest rate decision. [Senate Banking Committee, Apr 21, 2026]. STATUS Apr 24: CONFIRMATION STILL BLOCKED — Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) remains a 'no' vote, demanding the DOJ drop its investigation into outgoing Chair Powell first. With Republicans holding only a 12-10 advantage on the Banking Committee, one dissent blocks committee passage. No path to confirm before Powell's May 15 departure is visible. Warsh pledged 'regime change' at the Fed — fewer FOMC meetings, new inflation framework, and greater transparency. FOMC meets April 28-29 (4 days away) under Powell — likely his final meeting as Chair.
